normalized_title: Position Statement: Residential Accommodation for Ageing Persons
scope_summary: WAPC position statement setting requirements to support provision of residential accommodation for ageing persons through WA's local government planning framework. It applies to preparation and assessment of strategic and statutory planning proposals state-wide (local planning strategies, schemes and amendments, structure plans, subdivision and development applications). It requires local planning strategies to assess aged-housing demand, introduces standard land-use definitions ('residential aged care facility' and 'independent living complex') with permitted ('P') status in residential, mixed-use and centre zones, and directs how development applications for these uses are assessed, including interaction with R-Codes density bonuses.

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: 'residential aged care facility' and 'independent living complex' in residential and mixed use/centre type zones (X prohibited in industrial zones with sensitive land use conflict)
residential dwelling components of multi-storey independent living complex or residential aged care facility proposals, to be assessed under R-Codes Volume 2 - Apartments

key_numeric_standards.topic: aged/dependent persons dwelling density bonus (cited standard)
key_numeric_standards.value: density bonus per R-Codes Volume 1 deemed-to-comply Part D C1.1.5-C1.1.7
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: aged or dependent persons' dwellings and accessible dwellings; may be applied to eligible independent living complex components
key_numeric_standards.clause_ref: s 5.2 (citing R-Codes Vol 1 Part D, C1.1.5-C1.1.7)

cross_references.cited: State Planning Policy 7.0 Design of the Built Environment
clause 67 of the deemed provisions (Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015)
